metadata.title: Three-Statement Financial Model: Complete Machine-Readable Rulebook
metadata.description: Exhaustive, machine-readable specification defining every rule, relationship, linkage, constraint, dependency, and diagnostic governing the construction of a structurally correct three-statement financial model. Designed for AI agents and developers to implement a model builder producing mathematically sound and auditable output.
metadata.architecture_philosophy: Treat the model as a directed acyclic graph (DAG), not a spreadsheet. Isolate strongly connected components (SCCs) for iterative solving. Cash is solved last. The balance sheet is the single source of truth.
metadata.evaluation_order: column-wise (period-by-period), left-to-right across time, top-to-bottom within each period via topological sort

tolerance.description: Computation tolerance for iterative solvers and constraint checks. Display tolerance for human-readable output rounding.

foundational_accounting_identities.name: Accounting Equation
foundational_accounting_identities.violation_means: Broken linkage, sign error, or omitted transaction. The model is structurally invalid.
violation_implications: At least one cross-statement link is broken or has an incorrect sign
A transaction was recorded to only one side of the equation
A supporting schedule is not properly tied to the balance sheet
Cash, retained earnings, or a roll-forward schedule is disconnected
common_fixes: Re-tie cash from CFS ending cash to BS cash line
Verify retained earnings bridge (NI, dividends, adjustments)
Audit all supporting schedule ending balances vs BS line items
Check sign conventions on contra accounts (accumulated depreciation, treasury stock)
diagnostic_procedure: Step 1: Check cash tie-out (CFS ending cash vs BS cash)
Step 3: Check working capital deltas (CFS vs BS)
Step 4: Audit PP&E and debt roll-forwards for omitted non-cash items
edge_cases: Negative equity is permitted (accumulated deficit > APIC + common stock)
Non-controlling interests must be included in equity
Treasury stock is a contra-equity (negative) account
Unrealized gains/losses in AOCI affect equity but not retained earnings

foundational_accounting_identities.name: Retained Earnings Bridge

opening_balance.description: Retained earnings at the end of the prior period

net_income.description: Net income flows from IS bottom line to retained earnings

dividends.description: Common and preferred dividends declared reduce retained earnings

adjustments.description: Other items that bypass the income statement but affect retained earnings

items.name: Cumulative effect of accounting changes
items.description: Retrospective adjustments for changes in accounting principle

items.name: Prior period error corrections
items.description: Corrections of material errors in previously issued financial statements

items.name: Transition adjustments
items.description: One-time adjustments from adopting new accounting standards (e.g., ASC 842 lease transition)

foundational_accounting_identities.violation_means: Cumulative profits not correctly reconciled between IS and BS
common_fixes: Re-link Net Income from IS to RE bridge
Pull dividends correctly from CFF section
Check for omitted prior-period adjustments or accounting change effects
Ensure OCI items are flowing to AOCI, not retained earnings
foundational_accounting_identities.important_distinction: Other Comprehensive Income (OCI) items do NOT flow through retained earnings. They flow to Accumulated Other Comprehensive Income (AOCI), a separate equity component. Common OCI items include: unrealized gains/losses on AFS securities, foreign currency translation adjustments (CTA), pension/OPEB adjustments, and cash flow hedge gains/losses.

foundational_accounting_identities.name: Cash Bridge
foundational_accounting_identities.equation: Cash_t = Cash_{t-1} + CFO_t + CFI_t + CFF_t +/- FX_t
foundational_accounting_identities.latex: Cash_t = Cash_{t-1} + CFO_t + CFI_t + CFF_t \pm FX_t
foundational_accounting_identities.scope: Cash Flow Statement to Balance Sheet

opening_cash.description: Cash and cash equivalents at start of period

cfo.description: Net cash from operating activities (indirect method: NI + non-cash adjustments + WC changes)

cfi.description: Net cash from investing activities (CapEx, acquisitions, asset sales, investment purchases/sales)

cff.description: Net cash from financing activities (debt issuances/repayments, equity issuances/buybacks, dividends)

fx_effect.description: Effect of exchange rate changes on cash held in foreign currencies. Separate line item required by ASC 830/IAS 21.
fx_effect.mechanics: Difference between translating foreign-currency cash at beginning vs ending exchange rates

foundational_accounting_identities.violation_means: CFS does not accurately explain the change in BS cash
common_fixes: Fix non-cash adjustment signs in CFO
Fix working capital delta signs
Link BS cash directly to CFS ending cash (not an independent calculation)
Ensure FX effect on cash is properly isolated
Check that all investing and financing activities are captured

foundational_accounting_identities.name: PP&E Roll-Forward
foundational_accounting_identities.equation: PP&E_net_t = PP&E_net_{t-1} + CapEx_t - D&A_t - NBV_disposals_t +/- Impairment_t
foundational_accounting_identities.latex: PPE^{net}_t = PPE^{net}_{t-1} + CapEx_t - DA_t - NBV^{disp}_t - Impairment_t
foundational_accounting_identities.scope: Income Statement, Cash Flow Statement, and Balance Sheet

gross_ppe.description: Track additions at cost and removals at original cost

accumulated_depreciation.description: Track cumulative depreciation, removing accumulated depreciation on disposed assets

net_ppe.description: Net book value is gross cost less accumulated depreciation

foundational_accounting_identities.violation_means: Change in fixed asset base is incorrectly accounted for
common_fixes: Track gross PP&E and accumulated depreciation separately
Ensure disposal removes both gross cost AND accumulated depreciation
Verify CapEx ties to CFI outflows
Check that impairment charges are properly reflected
Confirm D&A from the PP&E schedule matches IS D&A and CFS add-back

foundational_accounting_identities.name: Debt Roll-Forward
foundational_accounting_identities.equation: Debt_t = Debt_{t-1} + Issuances_t - Repayments_t + PIK_t - Amortization_t
foundational_accounting_identities.latex: Debt_t = Debt_{t-1} + Iss_t - Repay_t + PIK_t - Amort_t
foundational_accounting_identities.scope: Cash Flow Statement (Financing) to Balance Sheet

components.opening_balance: Prior period ending balance for the tranche
components.issuances: New debt raised, flows through CFF as inflow (positive)
components.repayments: Mandatory amortization + optional prepayments, flows through CFF as outflow (negative)
components.pik_interest: Payment-in-Kind interest added to principal (non-cash, add back in CFO)
components.oid_amortization: Original Issue Discount amortization increases carrying value toward par
components.debt_issuance_cost_amort: Reduces the carrying value contra-liability; non-cash expense added back in CFO

foundational_accounting_identities.violation_means: Financing activities are not correctly linked to the BS
common_fixes: Use tranche-level schedules with explicit roll-forwards
Tie issuances and repayments to CFF
Include PIK interest as non-cash addition to principal
Account for OID amortization and debt issuance cost amortization
Ensure mandatory amortization schedules are correctly programmed

foundational_accounting_identities.name: EBITDA Bridge

adjusted_ebitda.equation: Adj_EBITDA = EBITDA + SBC + Restructuring + Impairment + Other_NonRecurring

common_addbacks.rationale: Non-cash expense; widely added back though debated

foundational_accounting_identities.violation_means: Logical impossibility — D&A cannot be negative under normal operations
common_fixes: Normalize signs to ensure D&A is always a positive add-back to EBIT
Ensure single source of truth for D&A (from PP&E/intangible schedules)
Do not double-count D&A included within COGS vs a separate line

RULE.description: Defines calculation logic for a specific line item

schema.name: string (human-readable name)
schema.section: string (e.g., CFO, CFI, CFF, CurrentAssets, LongTermDebt)
schema.unit: string (ISO 4217 currency code or 'ratio' or 'days' or 'shares')
schema.time_index: string (t for current, lag(x, n) for prior periods)
dependencies: array of RULE/LINK IDs this depends on

LINK.description: Maps values between statements with typed relationships

relationship_types.equals: Direct 1:1 mapping, no transformation. Source value equals target value.
relationship_types.delta: Target is the period-over-period change in the source. Used for working capital changes.
relationship_types.derived: Target is calculated from source using a formula. Used for interest expense from average debt.
relationship_types.roll_forward: Target ending balance = opening + additions - removals. Used for PP&E, debt, equity.

CONSTRAINT.description: Boolean predicate that must hold true in every period

schema.name: string (human-readable name)
schema.predicate: string (boolean expression that must evaluate to true)
schema.on_violation: string (action to take: HALT | WARN | LOG)

SEQ.description: Orchestrates evaluation order and manages dependencies

schema.name: string (human-readable name)
depends_on: array of SEQ IDs that must complete first
rules_to_evaluate: array of RULE IDs to evaluate in this phase

iteration_policy.is_scc: boolean (true if this phase contains circular references)
iteration_policy.breaker_mode: boolean (use beginning balances instead of iterating)

DIAG.description: Defines diagnostic tests with error messages and remediation steps

schema.name: string (human-readable name)
schema.check_formula: string (expression that should evaluate to true/within tolerance)
localization_steps: array of steps to find the error
fix_steps: array of steps to fix the error

expression_language.name: Financial Model DSL
expression_language.serialization: JSON-safe infix notation or explicit AST

special_functions.lag: lag(variable, periods) — references a prior period value
special_functions.lead: lead(variable, periods) — references a future period value (use with caution)
special_functions.avg_balance: avg_balance(open, close) = (open + close) / 2 — for interest calculations
special_functions.annualize: annualize(value, days_in_period, days_in_year) — for partial-year calculations
special_functions.days_in_period: days_in_period(t) — returns calendar days in the period
special_functions.coalesce: coalesce(a, b) — returns first non-null value

unit_checking.dimensional_analysis: Ensures units are consistent across operations (e.g., USD * ratio = USD, USD / USD = ratio)

inter_statement_linkages.name: Net Income to CFO

source.description: Bottom line of the income statement after all expenses, interest, and taxes

target.description: Starting point for indirect method cash flow from operations

inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: Positive NI is positive starting point. Losses are negative starting point.
inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: CFO will be incorrect by the full amount of net income, causing cash and BS to break

inter_statement_linkages.name: Net Income to Retained Earnings
inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: NI positive increases RE. Dividends (positive value) decrease RE.
inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: BS equity will be wrong, causing A != L + E imbalance

inter_statement_linkages.name: D&A to CFO Add-Back

source.description: Non-cash expense that reduces operating income

target.description: Non-cash expense added back to net income in CFO

inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: D&A is negative on IS (expense), added back as positive on CFS. Single source of truth from PP&E/intangible schedule.
inter_statement_linkages.single_source_rule: D&A must come from a single schedule (PP&E + Intangibles). IS D&A and CFS add-back must reference the same source.
inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: D&A mismatch between IS and CFS, causing incorrect CFO and potentially breaking cash tie-out

inter_statement_linkages.name: CapEx to Gross PP&E

source.description: Capital expenditures as cash outflow in investing activities

target.description: Gross property, plant, and equipment on the balance sheet

inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: CapEx is negative on CFS (cash outflow) but positive addition to gross PP&E. Use absolute value when linking.

inter_statement_linkages.name: Working Capital Changes

formulas.explanation: Increase in operating asset = use of cash (negative). Increase in operating liability = source of cash (positive).

accounts_receivable.logic: AR increase means revenue was earned but cash not yet collected = use of cash

inventory.logic: Inventory increase means cash was spent to build inventory = use of cash

prepaid_expenses.logic: Prepaid increase means cash paid in advance of expense recognition = use of cash

accounts_payable.logic: AP increase means expenses incurred but cash not yet paid = source of cash

accrued_liabilities.logic: Accrued liability increase means expense recognized but cash not yet paid = source of cash

deferred_revenue.logic: Deferred revenue increase means cash received before revenue recognized = source of cash

common_errors: Sign reversal — forgetting the negative sign for asset changes
Including non-operating items (e.g., changes in investment balances) in working capital
Not excluding acquisition-related balance changes from organic WC deltas
inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: CFO incorrect, cash tie-out fails, BS imbalance on cash line

inter_statement_linkages.name: Debt Flows to Debt Balances
inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: Issuances are positive CFF inflows. Repayments are negative CFF outflows. PIK is non-cash (not in CFF).
inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: Debt balance incorrect, BS imbalance, interest expense calculated on wrong base

inter_statement_linkages.name: Equity Flows to Equity Accounts
inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: Share repurchases are negative CFF outflows and increase treasury stock (contra-equity). Equity issuances are positive CFF inflows.

inter_statement_linkages.name: Average Debt to Interest Expense
inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: Interest expense is negative on IS (reduces earnings). Per-tranche calculation using tranche-specific rates.

day_count_conventions.30_360: Each month = 30 days, year = 360. Common for corporate bonds.
day_count_conventions.actual_360: Actual days in period / 360. Common for bank loans (SOFR-based).
day_count_conventions.actual_365: Actual days in period / 365. Common for some fixed-rate instruments.
day_count_conventions.actual_actual: Actual days / actual days in year. Government bonds.

inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: Interest expense wrong, NI wrong, RE wrong, BS imbalance

inter_statement_linkages.name: Ending Cash Tie-Out
inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: This must be an exact equality, not a calculated value. BS cash should reference CFS ending cash directly.
inter_statement_linkages.implementation_note: BS.Cash should be a direct cell reference to CFS.EndingCash, not an independent calculation. This ensures the tie-out is structural, not coincidental.
inter_statement_linkages.failure_symptom: If CFS ending cash != BS cash, the model is structurally broken

inter_statement_linkages.name: Stock-Based Compensation to CFO Add-Back
inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: SBC is negative on IS (expense), positive add-back on CFS, positive addition to APIC on BS.

three_statement_impact.IS: Operating expense reducing EBIT and Net Income

inter_statement_linkages.name: Deferred Tax to CFO Adjustment

source.description: Non-cash portion of total tax expense

inter_statement_linkages.sign_note: Deferred tax expense (increase in DTL or decrease in DTA) is a non-cash charge added back in CFO. Deferred tax benefit (decrease in DTL or increase in DTA) is subtracted.

inter_statement_linkages.name: Debt Schedule to BS
inter_statement_linkages.maturity_classification: Amounts due within 12 months classified as current portion of long-term debt

structure.description: Canonical income statement structure from revenue to net income

line_items.name: Revenue
forecast_methods: Volume x Price: Revenue_t = Units_t * ASP_t
Backlog conversion: Revenue_t = Backlog_{t-1} * ConversionRate + NewOrders_t * InPeriodConversion

line_items.name: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)
forecast_methods: Cost build-up: COGS_t = Materials_t + Labor_t + Overhead_t + D&A_inCOGS_t

line_items.name: Selling, General & Administrative (SG&A)
forecast_methods: Percent of revenue: SGA_t = Revenue_t * SGA%
Fixed + variable: SGA_t = FixedSGA + Revenue_t * VariableSGA%

line_items.name: Research & Development (R&D)
forecast_methods: Percent of revenue: RD_t = Revenue_t * RD%
line_items.gaap_ifrs_note: US GAAP expenses all R&D (ASC 730). IFRS capitalizes development costs meeting IAS 38 criteria.

line_items.name: Depreciation & Amortization (if separate line)
line_items.note: May be embedded in COGS and SG&A. If shown separately, ensure no double-counting.

line_items.name: Stock-Based Compensation (if separate line)
line_items.note: May be embedded in COGS, SG&A, R&D. If shown separately, ensure no double-counting.

line_items.name: Operating Income (EBIT)
line_items.formula: EBIT_t = GrossProfit_t + SGA_t + RD_t + DA_t + SBC_t + OtherOp_t

line_items.name: Interest Expense
line_items.source: Debt schedule (average balance * rate per tranche)

line_items.name: Interest Income
line_items.note: Often immaterial and can be set to zero or a simple assumption

cash_flow_statement_rules.description: The CFS reconciles net income to net cash generated. It is the most error-prone statement and must be constructed using strict rules.

structure.description: Add back non-cash expenses, subtract non-cash gains

items.name: Depreciation & Amortization
items.rationale: Non-cash expense that reduced NI but did not use cash

items.name: Deferred Income Taxes
items.sign_on_cfs: positive if expense, negative if benefit
items.rationale: Non-cash portion of tax expense (change in DTA/DTL)

items.name: Amortization of Debt Issuance Costs / OID
Gain on Sale of Assets
items.rationale: Non-cash gain included in NI; actual cash proceeds appear in CFI

items.name: Loss on Extinguishment of Debt
items.rationale: Non-cash component (write-off of unamortized issuance costs/OID)

items.name: PIK Interest Accrued
items.rationale: Non-cash interest added to principal, not paid in cash

structure.description: Changes in operating assets and liabilities derived from BS deltas

cfi.name: Cash Flow from Investing (CFI)

categories.maintenance_capex: Required to maintain existing asset base

items.name: Acquisitions
items.description: Net cash paid for business combinations

items.name: Proceeds from Asset Sales
items.source: Full cash proceeds, not just gain/loss
items.note: Gain/loss is reversed in CFO; full proceeds appear here

items.name: Purchases of Investments
items.description: Purchases of marketable securities, equity investments

items.name: Capitalized Software Development
items.gaap_ifrs_note: IFRS capitalizes qualifying development costs; US GAAP generally expenses (except ASC 350-40 for internal-use software)

cfi.total_formula: CFI_t = CapEx_t + Acquisitions_t + AssetSales_t + InvestmentPurchases_t + InvestmentSales_t + Other_t

cff.name: Cash Flow from Financing (CFF)

items.name: Revolver Draws / (Repayments)
items.sign: net (positive if draw, negative if repayment)
items.note: Often shown as net change in revolver balance

items.name: Debt Issuance Costs Paid
items.note: Cash outflow when debt is issued. Amortized as non-cash expense over debt life.

items.name: Payment of Finance Lease Obligations
items.note: Principal portion of finance lease payments (ASC 842)

cff.total_formula: CFF_t = DebtIssuances_t + DebtRepayments_t + RevolverNet_t + EquityIssuances_t + Buybacks_t + Dividends_t + Other_t

fx_effect.name: Effect of Exchange Rate Changes on Cash
fx_effect.description: Separate line showing impact of exchange rate changes on foreign-currency denominated cash balances

ending_cash.formula: EndingCash_t = BeginningCash_t + CFO_t + CFI_t + CFF_t + FX_Effect_t

fundamental_constraint.enforcement: Every period, every scenario. No exceptions.

current_assets.name: Cash and Cash Equivalents
current_assets.source: CFS.EndingCash (MUST be direct reference, not independent calculation)
current_assets.constraint: Cash >= 0 (if no revolver) or Cash >= MinCash (if revolver available)

current_assets.name: Other Current Assets
current_assets.forecast: Percent of revenue or held constant

non_current_assets.name: Goodwill
non_current_assets.note: Not amortized. Tested for impairment annually or when triggering events occur.

non_current_assets.name: Equity Method Investments
non_current_assets.forecast: Opening + Share of Income - Dividends Received

non_current_assets.name: Other Non-Current Assets
non_current_assets.forecast: Percent of revenue or held constant

current_liabilities.name: Current Portion of Long-Term Debt
current_liabilities.source: Debt Schedule (mandatory amortization due within 12 months)

current_liabilities.name: Other Current Liabilities
current_liabilities.forecast: Percent of revenue or held constant

non_current_liabilities.name: Long-Term Debt
non_current_liabilities.source: Debt Schedule (ending balance per tranche, net of current portion)
non_current_liabilities.presentation: Net of unamortized OID and debt issuance costs (ASC 835-30)

non_current_liabilities.name: Other Non-Current Liabilities
non_current_liabilities.forecast: Percent of revenue or held constant

components.name: Non-Controlling Interests (NCI)
components.note: Required for consolidated entities with minority shareholders

equity.total_equity_formula: TotalEquity_t = CommonStock_t + APIC_t + RE_t + AOCI_t + TreasuryStock_t + NCI_t

plug_hierarchy.description: Formal Financing Plug Policy determines how cash shortfalls are absorbed

logic.shortfall: If cash_pre_plug < MinCash, draw on revolver: Draw = min(MaxAvailability, MinCash - cash_pre_plug)
logic.excess: If cash_pre_plug > MinCash and RevolverBalance > 0, sweep: Sweep = min(RevolverBalance, cash_pre_plug - MinCash)

priority_order.condition: No revolver available or revolver fully drawn

logic.normal: Cash = CFS.EndingCash (can fluctuate freely above zero)
logic.negative_cash_warning: If Cash < 0, model indicates liquidity shortfall requiring explicit financing assumptions

revolver_solving_sequence: Step 1: Calculate pre-revolver cash (all CFS items except revolver draw/repay)
Step 2: Determine required revolver draw or sweep based on MinCash policy
Step 3: Constrain draw to available commitment
Step 4: Calculate interest on average revolver balance (creates circularity)
Step 5: Iterate to convergence (fixed-point iteration with damping)

cash_flow_impact.direction: Increase = Use of cash (Negative on CFS)

working_capital_mechanics.sensitivity: A 1-day increase in DSO on $1B revenue ≈ $2.74M additional cash tied up (assuming 365 days)

cash_flow_impact.direction: Increase = Use of cash (Negative on CFS)

working_capital_mechanics.note: COGS used as denominator (not revenue) because inventory is carried at cost

cash_flow_impact.direction: Increase = Source of cash (Positive on CFS)

working_capital_mechanics.note: COGS used as denominator for consistency with inventory

working_capital_mechanics.driver: Percent of SG&A or relevant expense base

cash_flow_impact.direction: Increase = Use of cash (Negative on CFS)

working_capital_mechanics.driver: Percent of relevant expense base (SG&A, total OpEx, or payroll)

cash_flow_impact.direction: Increase = Source of cash (Positive on CFS)
Increase = Source of cash (Positive on CFS)

asc_606_considerations: Revenue recognized when performance obligation is satisfied
Contract assets (unbilled receivables) arise when revenue > billings
Contract liabilities (deferred revenue) arise when billings > revenue

working_capital_mechanics.name: Cash Conversion Cycle
working_capital_mechanics.interpretation: Number of days between paying for inventory and collecting cash from customers
working_capital_mechanics.optimization: Lower CCC = better working capital efficiency. Negative CCC means company collects before paying (e.g., Amazon).

fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.name: PP&E Roll-Forward
fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.description: Property, Plant & Equipment must be modeled with separate roll-forwards for Gross PP&E and Accumulated Depreciation

components.acquired_assets: Fair value of PP&E from acquisitions (PPA)
components.disposals: Original cost of assets sold or retired

depreciation_methods.name: Straight-Line
depreciation_methods.usage: Most common. Required for financial reporting by many companies.

depreciation_methods.name: Double Declining Balance
depreciation_methods.usage: Accelerated method. Switches to straight-line when SL > DDB.

depreciation_methods.name: Units of Production
depreciation_methods.usage: Mining, oil & gas, manufacturing equipment with measurable output.

useful_life_ranges.leasehold_improvements: Shorter of useful life or lease term

disposal_accounting.step_1: Remove gross cost from Gross PP&E
disposal_accounting.step_2: Remove accumulated depreciation on disposed asset
disposal_accounting.step_3: Calculate gain/loss: Proceeds - NBV (where NBV = GrossCost - AccumDepr)
disposal_accounting.step_4: Record gain/loss on IS (other operating income/expense)
disposal_accounting.step_5: Record full cash proceeds in CFI (investing activities)
disposal_accounting.step_6: Reverse non-cash gain/loss in CFO (operating activities)

us_gaap.trigger: Events or changes in circumstances indicating carrying amount may not be recoverable
us_gaap.test: Step 1: Recoverability test (undiscounted cash flows vs carrying amount). Step 2: If fails, impairment = carrying amount - fair value.

ifrs.test: Impairment = carrying amount - recoverable amount (higher of fair value less costs to sell and value in use)
ifrs.reversal: Permitted for PP&E (not for goodwill). Reversal limited to original carrying amount less depreciation.

fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.fully_depreciated_assets: Remain on BS (gross cost and accumulated depreciation) until physically disposed of. NBV = 0. No further depreciation expense.

construction_in_progress.treatment: Capitalized as CIP on BS, not depreciated until asset is placed in service
construction_in_progress.capitalized_interest: Interest costs during construction may be capitalized (ASC 835-20)

indefinite_lived.treatment: NOT amortized. Tested for impairment annually.
indefinite_lived.impairment_test: Fair value compared to carrying amount

fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.name: Goodwill
fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.definition: Excess of purchase price over fair value of identifiable net assets acquired
fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.treatment: NOT amortized. Tested for impairment at least annually.

us_gaap.method: One-step test (ASU 2017-04): Impairment = CarryingAmount_ReportingUnit - FairValue_ReportingUnit, limited to goodwill allocated to that unit

ifrs.method: Allocated to cash-generating units (CGUs). Impairment if carrying amount of CGU > recoverable amount.

fixed_and_intangible_asset_rules.roll_forward: Goodwill_t = Goodwill_{t-1} + NewAcquisitions_t - Impairment_t - Disposals_t + FXAdjustments_t

current_tax.description: Taxes actually payable to taxing authorities for the current period
current_tax.cash_impact: Reduces cash. Reflected in CFO as cash taxes paid.

deferred_tax.description: Non-cash change in DTA/DTL balances arising from temporary differences
deferred_tax.cash_impact: Non-cash. Added back / subtracted in CFO.

taxation_rules.name: Deferred Tax Assets and Liabilities
taxation_rules.description: Arise from temporary differences between book and tax treatment of items

dta_sources.description: Tax losses that can offset future taxable income
Expenses recognized for book but not yet deductible for tax (e.g., warranty reserves, litigation accruals)
Cash received and taxable but not yet recognized as book revenue
Book expense recognized over vesting; tax deduction at exercise/vesting
Book expense for bad debts; tax deduction when actually written off
ASC 842 lease liability creates a DTA when liability > ROU asset
R&D credits, foreign tax credits not yet utilized

dtl_sources.description: Tax depreciation (MACRS) faster than book depreciation (straight-line)
Deducted for tax when paid; recognized as expense over time for book
Revenue recognized for book at sale; taxed as cash collected
Capitalized for book; may be deducted currently for tax
Amortizable for tax (Section 197); may have different book treatment
ROU asset > lease liability can create DTL

permanent_differences.description: Items that differ between book and tax permanently (never reverse)
examples: Goodwill impairment (if tax goodwill basis differs)
permanent_differences.impact: Cause effective tax rate to differ from statutory rate

taxation_rules.name: Net Operating Loss (NOL) Carryforward
taxation_rules.description: Tax losses that can be carried forward to offset future taxable income

pre_tcja_rules.note: CARES Act (2020) temporarily allowed 5-year carryback for 2018-2020 NOLs

post_tcja_rules.carryback: Not permitted (with exceptions for farming losses)
post_tcja_rules.limitation: Limited to 80% of taxable income in any given year

vintage_tracking.description: Track NOLs by vintage year to properly apply pre/post-TCJA rules and expiration

section_382_limitation.trigger: Greater than 50% ownership change within a 3-year testing period
section_382_limitation.description: Limits the annual use of pre-change NOLs to prevent trafficking in loss companies
section_382_limitation.modeling_impact: Must track Section 382 limitation separately from the 80% TCJA limitation

balance_sheet_impact.dta: NOL carryforward creates a DTA at the statutory tax rate
balance_sheet_impact.valuation_allowance: Required if more likely than not (>50%) that some or all DTA will not be realized

cash_flow_impact.description: NOL utilization reduces current cash taxes paid
cash_flow_impact.cfo_effect: Increases CFO by reducing cash tax payments

taxation_rules.name: Valuation Allowance
taxation_rules.description: Contra-asset recorded against DTA if it is more likely than not (>50% probability) that some or all DTA will not be realized

negative_evidence: Cumulative losses in recent years (3-year cumulative loss is strong negative evidence)
Losses expected in early future years
Unsettled circumstances that may adversely affect future operations
positive_evidence: Existing contracts or firm sales backlog
Excess of appreciated asset FV over tax basis
Strong earnings history exclusive of the loss that created the DTA
Tax planning strategies that would accelerate taxable income

taxation_rules.modeling_impact: Change in VA hits tax expense (IS) and DTA (BS). Increasing VA increases tax expense; decreasing VA decreases tax expense.

taxation_rules.name: Tax Rate Reconciliation

statutory_rate.description: Post-TCJA (2018+) US federal corporate income tax rate

reconciliation_items: State and local income taxes (net of federal benefit)

gilti.name: Global Intangible Low-Taxed Income
gilti.description: Minimum tax on foreign earnings exceeding 10% return on tangible assets

beat.name: Base Erosion and Anti-Abuse Tax
beat.description: Alternative minimum tax on certain payments to foreign related parties

fdii.name: Foreign-Derived Intangible Income
fdii.description: Reduced rate on income from serving foreign markets

debt_and_financing_rules.name: Revolving Credit Facility
debt_and_financing_rules.description: The revolver acts as the primary financing plug, absorbing cash shortfalls and sweeping excess cash

key_formulas.draw_logic: If cash_pre_plug_t < MinCash: Draw_t = min(MaxAvailability_t - Drawn_{t-1}, MinCash - cash_pre_plug_t)
key_formulas.sweep_logic: If cash_pre_plug_t > MinCash and Drawn_{t-1} > 0: Sweep_t = min(Drawn_{t-1}, cash_pre_plug_t - MinCash)

commitment_fee.description: Fee on undrawn portion of the commitment
commitment_fee.cross_statement: IS: Other expense below EBIT or within interest. CFS: Included in CFO.

cross_statement_impact.IS: Interest expense on drawn balance + commitment fee on undrawn balance
cross_statement_impact.CFS_CFF: Net draw/sweep flows through financing activities
cross_statement_impact.BS: Current liability (typically classified as current due to revolver nature)

debt_and_financing_rules.name: Term Loan / Bond Tranche Schedule
debt_and_financing_rules.description: Each debt tranche requires its own roll-forward schedule

roll_forward.formula: Closing_t = Opening_t + NewIssuances_t + PIK_t - MandatoryAmort_t - OptionalPrepay_t

components.new_issuances: New debt raised at par (or net of OID)
components.mandatory_amortization: Scheduled principal payments per credit agreement
components.maturity_repayment: Bullet payment at maturity of remaining balance

term_loan_a.description: Amortizing term loan, typically held by banks
term_loan_a.amortization: Quarterly mandatory amortization (5-10% per year), bullet at maturity

term_loan_b.amortization: 1% annual mandatory amortization, bullet at maturity
term_loan_b.excess_cash_flow_sweep: 25-75% of excess cash flow (with leverage-based step-downs)

senior_secured_notes.call_protection: Non-call period (2-5 years), then callable at premium declining to par

subordinated_notes.description: Junior debt, higher risk, higher return

mezzanine_debt.description: Subordinated debt, often with equity kicker (warrants)

floating_rate.note: SOFR replaced LIBOR as of June 30, 2023
floating_rate.floor: Many loans have SOFR floors (e.g., 0.50% or 1.00%)

cross_statement.CFS_CFO: Added back as non-cash charge in CFO

oid.description: Original Issue Discount presented as contra-liability reducing carrying value
oid.amortization: Effective interest method over life of debt
oid.cross_statement: IS: Part of interest expense. CFS: Added back in CFO (non-cash). BS: Increases carrying value toward par.

debt_issuance_costs.description: Presented as direct deduction from debt carrying amount (not a separate asset)
debt_issuance_costs.cross_statement: IS: Part of interest expense. CFS: Added back in CFO (non-cash). BS: Increases carrying value toward par.

debt_and_financing_rules.name: Financial Covenant Testing
debt_and_financing_rules.description: Covenants must be tested each period as constraints

common_covenants.name: Maximum Leverage Ratio
common_covenants.notes: Often uses 'Adjusted EBITDA' per credit agreement definition

modeling_as_constraint.on_violation: Flag for user review; do not auto-cure

debt_and_financing_rules.name: Excess Cash Flow Sweep
debt_and_financing_rules.description: Mandatory prepayment from excess cash flow, common in leveraged loans

formula.excess_cash_flow: ECF = EBITDA - CashInterest - CashTaxes - CapEx - MandatoryAmort - WC_Increase

debt_and_financing_rules.application: Applied against term loan balance in order of maturity (TLA before TLB)

equity_and_eps_rules.name: Equity Roll-Forward
equity_and_eps_rules.description: Reconcile each equity component period over period

common_stock.note: Typically immaterial due to low par values

apic.roll_forward: APIC_t = APIC_{t-1} + EquityIssuanceExcessPar_t + SBC_Expense_t + OptionExerciseExcess_t - ExcessTaxBenefitPreASU2016_09

retained_earnings.roll_forward: RE_t = RE_{t-1} + NetIncome_t - CommonDividends_t - PreferredDividends_t +/- CumulativeAdjustments_t
retained_earnings.important: This is the primary link between IS and BS equity

oci_components: Unrealized gains/losses on AFS debt securities
Credit losses on AFS debt securities

treasury_stock.sign: Contra-equity (negative). Repurchases increase the contra (more negative).
treasury_stock.method: Cost method (most common): Record at repurchase cost
treasury_stock.cost_method_note: Under cost method, treasury stock debited at repurchase price. On reissuance, if price > cost, excess goes to APIC. If price < cost, difference reduces APIC then RE.

equity_and_eps_rules.total_equity: TotalEquity_t = CommonStock_t + APIC_t + RE_t + AOCI_t + TreasuryStock_t + NCI_t

equity_and_eps_rules.name: Stock-Based Compensation
equity_and_eps_rules.description: SBC is a non-cash operating expense with specific three-statement impacts

measurement.rsus_valuation: Stock price on grant date * number of units
measurement.performance_based: Probability-weighted fair value based on expected achievement

vesting_schedule.performance_based: Vesting contingent on financial or operational metrics
vesting_schedule.market_based: Vesting contingent on stock price targets (TSR awards)

three_statement_impact.IS: SBC_expense reduces operating income. May be allocated across COGS, R&D, SG&A.
three_statement_impact.CFS_CFO: Added back as non-cash charge (positive adjustment to CFO)

forfeitures.us_gaap: ASC 718: Policy election to (a) estimate forfeitures at grant or (b) account for forfeitures as they occur
forfeitures.ifrs: IFRS 2: Must estimate forfeitures at grant date and update estimate each period

equity_and_eps_rules.name: Share Count Roll-Forward
equity_and_eps_rules.description: Track basic and diluted shares outstanding

basic_shares.roll_forward: BasicShares_t = BasicShares_{t-1} + NewIssuances_t + OptionExercises_t + RSU_Vesting_t - Repurchases_t
basic_shares.weighted_average: Weight each change by the fraction of the period it was outstanding

diluted_shares.description: Pro-forma calculation including all potentially dilutive securities

conditions: Only include in-the-money options (exercise price < average stock price)
Assumes exercise proceeds used to repurchase shares at average market price
treasury_stock_method.rsu_treatment: Unvested RSUs treated as outstanding shares. Proceeds = unrecognized compensation cost + excess tax benefits.

formula.numerator_adjustment: Add back interest expense (net of tax) for convertible debt, or add back preferred dividends for convertible preferred

conditions: Only included if effect is dilutive (reduces EPS)
Assumes conversion at beginning of period (or issuance date if later)
Each potentially dilutive security tested independently

diluted_shares.anti_dilution_test: Each potentially dilutive security is tested independently. If including it increases EPS (anti-dilutive), exclude it from diluted EPS calculation.

numerator_adjustments: Add back convertible preferred dividends (if dilutive)
Add back convertible debt interest, net of tax (if dilutive)
denominator_adjustments: Add dilutive shares from TSM (options, warrants, RSUs)
Add conversion shares from if-converted method (convertible debt, preferred)

equity_and_eps_rules.name: Share Repurchase Program
equity_and_eps_rules.description: Modeling share buybacks and their three-statement impact

methods.name: Dollar-amount driven
methods.use_when: Company has announced a fixed dollar repurchase program

methods.name: Excess cash driven
methods.formula: BuybackBudget_t = max(0, FreeCashFlow_t - Dividends_t - DebtRepay_t - MinCash_t)
methods.use_when: Buybacks are a residual use of excess cash

three_statement_impact.IS: No direct impact (repurchases do not appear on IS). Reduces future EPS denominators.
three_statement_impact.BS: Increases treasury stock (contra-equity, more negative). Reduces total equity.

sign_convention_rules.description: Strict sign conventions must be normalized at data ingestion to prevent systemic double-counting and working capital reversals

income_statement.convention: Revenues and gains positive; expenses and losses negative
income_statement.alternative_convention: Some models show expenses as positive absolute values with explicit subtraction. The canonical convention treats all values as signed for consistent aggregation.
income_statement.normalization_rule: At ingestion, convert all IS items to signed convention. If source data shows COGS as positive $60M, convert to -$60M.

balance_sheet.convention: Assets positive; Liabilities positive; Equity positive; Contra accounts negative
balance_sheet.normalization_rule: Contra accounts (Accumulated Depreciation, Treasury Stock, Valuation Allowance) carry negative signs to allow direct summation.

cash_flow_statement.convention: Cash inflows positive; Cash outflows negative
cash_flow_statement.normalization_rule: Outflows are always negative regardless of how displayed in source financials.

common_sign_errors.description: Treating increase in AR as positive on CFS instead of negative
common_sign_errors.impact: CFO overstated by 2x the WC change, cash balance wrong

common_sign_errors.description: Treating accumulated depreciation as positive then subtracting it
common_sign_errors.fix: Store AccumDepr as negative; NetPPE = GrossPPE + AccumDepr (addition with negative value = subtraction)

common_sign_errors.error: CapEx sign on CFS vs PP&E schedule
common_sign_errors.description: CapEx negative on CFS (outflow) but must be positive addition to Gross PP&E
common_sign_errors.impact: PP&E decreasing when it should increase
common_sign_errors.fix: Use abs(CFS.CapEx) when rolling forward Gross PP&E

common_sign_errors.description: D&A is negative on IS but must be positive on CFS
Gain is positive on IS, must be negative adjustment in CFO (reverse it), and full proceeds are positive in CFI
common_sign_errors.fix: CFO: subtract gain (or add back loss). CFI: record full cash proceeds.

circularity_handling.name: Interest-Cash-Revolver Loop
dependency_loop: Average debt balance drives interest expense
Revolver draw/sweep changes average debt balance

steps: Step 1: Initialize revolver balance to zero (or prior period balance)
Step 2: Calculate interest expense based on current debt balances
Step 3: Calculate NI, CFO, and pre-plug cash
Step 4: Determine revolver draw/sweep based on pre-plug cash vs min cash
Step 5: Apply damping: new_balance = 0.7 * calculated_balance + 0.3 * previous_iteration_balance
Step 7: If not converged, repeat from Step 2

breaker_mode.description: Use beginning-of-period balances for interest calculation, breaking the circularity
breaker_mode.formula: IS.InterestExp_t = BS.DebtBegin_t * Rate (instead of average balance)
when_to_use: Sensitivity tables where iteration would be too slow
Models where revolver draws are immaterial
breaker_mode.tradeoff: Less precise but eliminates iteration entirely

circularity_handling.name: Tax-Interest-NOL Loop
dependency_loop: NOL utilization affects current tax expense

resolution.description: This loop is part of the Interest-Cash-Revolver SCC and is solved implicitly within the same fixed-point iteration
resolution.note: The NOL utilization adds complexity but does not create a separate SCC — it is nested within the primary loop

circularity_handling.name: SBC-EPS Target Loop
dependency_loop: EPS target drives performance-based SBC vesting probability
EPS determines if performance targets are met
Performance achievement affects SBC vesting and future expense

resolution.description: Separate iteration loop after the primary Interest-Revolver loop has converged

circularity_handling.when_relevant: Only for models with material performance-based equity awards where EPS is a vesting metric

model_sequencing.description: Solve period-by-period (left to right). Within each period, follow topological sort order.

canonical_build_order.name: Income Statement — Operating Lines
canonical_build_order.output: EBIT (before D&A if shown separately, else after)

sub_phases.name: PP&E and Intangibles Schedule
sub_phases.outputs_to: IS (D&A expense), CFS (CapEx, D&A add-back), BS (Net PP&E, Net Intangibles)

sub_phases.name: Debt Schedule
sub_phases.depends_on: Prior period balances, new issuance assumptions
sub_phases.outputs_to: IS (Interest expense), CFS (debt issuances/repayments, interest paid), BS (debt balances)
sub_phases.note: Interest expense depends on average balance which depends on revolver which depends on cash which depends on NI which depends on interest

sub_phases.name: Tax Schedule
sub_phases.outputs_to: IS (tax expense), CFS (deferred tax add-back), BS (DTA, DTL, taxes payable)

sub_phases.name: Equity / SBC Schedule
sub_phases.outputs_to: IS (SBC expense), CFS (SBC add-back, buyback outflows), BS (APIC, Treasury Stock, Share Count)

canonical_build_order.name: Cash Flow Statement
canonical_build_order.depends_on: Phase 3 (NI), Phase 2a (WC), Phase 2b (D&A, CapEx), Phase 2c (Debt flows), Phase 2e (Buybacks)

canonical_build_order.name: Balance Sheet
items: All BS line items pulled from respective schedules
process: Pull current assets (ex-cash) from WC schedule
Pull non-current assets from PP&E/intangibles/goodwill schedules
Pull liabilities from debt schedule and WC schedule
Pull equity from equity schedule and RE bridge

canonical_build_order.name: Diagnostic Checks
items: All DIAG tests run after BS is complete

iteration_phase.description: If the model has circular references (Interest-Revolver), Phases 2c through 5 are wrapped in a fixed-point iteration loop

diagnostics.name: Balance Sheet Balance Check
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Broken link or sign error in cross-statement flow
localization_steps: Verify all schedule ending balances tied to BS
diagnostics.fix: Re-link cash and retained earnings. Audit supporting schedules.

diagnostics.name: Cash Tie-Out
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: BS cash is independently calculated instead of referencing CFS ending cash
diagnostics.fix: Set BS.Cash = CFS.EndingCash as a direct reference

diagnostics.name: Retained Earnings Continuity
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Missing NI link or omitted dividends in RE bridge
diagnostics.fix: Re-link IS.NetIncome to RE bridge. Verify dividends from CFF.

diagnostics.name: Working Capital Delta Matching
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Working capital sign reversal or omitted line item
diagnostics.fix: Normalize signs. Ensure each WC line uses the delta formula consistently.

diagnostics.name: D&A Consistency
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Multiple D&A sources (IS has different value than CFS add-back)
diagnostics.fix: Use single D&A schedule feeding both IS and CFS

diagnostics.name: Debt Roll-Forward Integrity
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Missing PIK accrual or CFF flow not tied to schedule
diagnostics.fix: Ensure tranche schedule ties to CFF for cash flows and BS for ending balances

diagnostics.name: PP&E Roll-Forward Integrity
diagnostics.check_formula: abs(NetPPE_t - (NetPPE_{t-1} + CapEx_t - DA_t - NBV_Disposals_t - Impairment_t)) <= tolerance
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Disposal NBV not removed from both gross and accumulated depreciation
diagnostics.fix: Verify disposal accounting removes both gross cost and accumulated depreciation

diagnostics.name: Total Equity Reconciliation
diagnostics.check_formula: abs(TotalEquity_t - (CommonStock_t + APIC_t + RE_t + AOCI_t + TreasuryStock_t + NCI_t)) <= tolerance
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Missing equity component or SBC not flowing to APIC
diagnostics.fix: Verify all equity components are included and roll-forwards are correct

diagnostics.name: Circularity Convergence Check
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Model is diverging — check for explosive feedback loops or incorrect formulas
diagnostics.fix: Check damping factor, review formula logic, consider breaker mode

diagnostics.name: Negative Cash / Liquidity Check
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Insufficient financing assumptions; revolver fully drawn and cash still negative
diagnostics.fix: Review financing assumptions. Consider additional debt/equity issuance or reduced spending.

diagnostics.name: Financial Covenant Compliance
diagnostics.typical_root_cause: Operating performance deterioration or excessive leverage
diagnostics.fix: Flag for user review; may require revised operating assumptions or additional equity

negative_equity.description: Accumulated deficit exceeds APIC and common stock, resulting in negative total equity
negative_equity.modeling_rule: Allowed. Do not net accumulated deficits against APIC. Report each component separately.
negative_equity.bs_impact: TotalEquity can be negative. A = L + E still holds (E is negative, so L > A).
negative_equity.real_world: Common in early-stage companies, highly leveraged companies, and companies with large share repurchase programs

acquisitions_ppa.description: Purchase Price Allocation in business combinations
steps: Step 1: Determine total consideration (cash + stock + contingent consideration)
Step 2: Identify and measure fair value of all identifiable assets and liabilities
Step 3: Step up/down assets to fair value (creates new tax basis differences → DTAs/DTLs)
Step 4: Calculate goodwill = Consideration - Fair Value of Net Identifiable Assets
Step 5: Establish new intangible assets (customer relationships, technology, trade names)
Step 6: Record new DTAs/DTLs from fair value step-ups/step-downs

three_statement_impact.BS: New assets at fair value, new intangibles, goodwill, acquisition DTAs/DTLs
three_statement_impact.IS: New amortization expense from acquired intangibles; transaction costs expensed immediately (ASC 805)
three_statement_impact.CFS_CFI: Cash consideration as investing outflow, net of cash acquired

discontinued_operations.description: Business components that have been sold, abandoned, or classified as held for sale
modeling_rules: Segregate discontinued operations from continuing operations on IS
Report as single line below income from continuing operations
Restate prior periods to reclassify discontinued ops
On BS, classify assets/liabilities as held for sale (current)
Gain/loss on disposal reported in discontinued operations

foreign_currency_translation.description: Translation of foreign subsidiary financial statements into reporting currency

rules.income_statement: Translate at average exchange rate for the period
rules.equity: Translate at historical rates (rates when equity transactions occurred)
rules.imbalance: The difference goes to Cumulative Translation Adjustment (CTA) in AOCI (equity)

foreign_currency_translation.cash_flow_impact: Separate line on CFS: 'Effect of exchange rate changes on cash'

hyperinflationary_economies.description: Financial statements of entities with functional currency of a hyperinflationary economy must be restated
hyperinflationary_economies.ifrs_treatment: Restate non-monetary items to current purchasing power using general price index
hyperinflationary_economies.us_gaap_treatment: Use reporting currency (USD) as functional currency (remeasurement)

non_controlling_interests.description: Minority shareholders' interest in consolidated subsidiaries
non_controlling_interests.is_presentation: Net income allocated between parent and NCI. NCI share shown below consolidated NI.

equity_method_investments.description: Investments where company has significant influence (typically 20-50% ownership)
equity_method_investments.bs_treatment: Single line asset: Investment_t = Investment_{t-1} + ShareOfNI_t - Dividends_t +/- OCI_t
equity_method_investments.is_treatment: Single line: Equity in earnings of investee (below operating income)
equity_method_investments.cfs_treatment: Share of NI is non-cash in CFO (added back or subtracted). Dividends received in CFO or CFI.

key_difference.us_gaap: Dual model: Operating leases (straight-line expense) and Finance leases (front-loaded expense like debt)
key_difference.ifrs: Single model: All leases treated like finance leases (except short-term and low-value exemptions)

operating_lease_us_gaap.BS: ROU Asset and Lease Liability (both sides of BS)
operating_lease_us_gaap.IS: Single straight-line lease expense in operating expenses

finance_lease.BS: ROU Asset (amortized) and Lease Liability (reduced by payments)
finance_lease.IS: Amortization of ROU asset (operating) + Interest on lease liability (below EBIT)
finance_lease.CFS: Interest portion in CFO, Principal portion in CFF

restructuring_charges.description: One-time costs for workforce reductions, facility closures, contract terminations
modeling_rules: Record as separate operating expense line on IS
Non-cash portion (asset impairments) added back in CFO
Cash portion (severance, termination fees) reflected in CFO as cash used

gaap_vs_ifrs_crosswalk.description: Policy flags to handle standard differences without rewriting calculations

rules.income_taxes_paid: CFO (required, unless specifically identified with CFI or CFF)

policy_flags.routing_logic: Route interest/dividend cash flows to appropriate CFS section based on flag

us_gaap.model: Dual model: Operating leases and Finance leases

finance_lease.cfs: Interest in CFO, Principal in CFF

ifrs.model: Single model: All leases treated as finance leases
ifrs.exceptions: Short-term leases (<12 months) and low-value assets may be expensed straight-line

all_leases.is: Depreciation of ROU asset + Interest on lease liability
all_leases.cfs: Interest in CFO, Principal in CFF

policy_flags.impact: IFRS models show higher EBITDA (lease expense reclassified to depreciation + interest) and higher CFO (principal payments in CFF)

us_gaap.exception: Software development costs capitalized after technological feasibility (ASC 985-20) or for internal use (ASC 350-40)

ifrs.rule: Research phase: Expense. Development phase: Capitalize if all six criteria are met.
six_criteria: Technical feasibility of completing the asset
How the asset will generate probable future economic benefits

policy_flags.impact: Capitalization creates intangible asset on BS, reduces IS expense in current period, adds amortization in future periods

us_gaap.exception: Assets held for sale can be written back up to carrying amount before held-for-sale classification

ifrs.reversal: Permitted for PP&E and finite-lived intangibles (up to original carrying amount less normal depreciation). NOT permitted for goodwill.
ifrs.reversal_limit: Cannot exceed what carrying amount would have been without impairment (after normal depreciation)

policy_flags.impact: IFRS models may show income from reversal of previous impairment charges

us_gaap.rule: Policy election: (a) Estimate forfeitures at grant date and revise, OR (b) Account for forfeitures as they occur (ASU 2016-09)

ifrs.rule: Must estimate forfeitures at grant date and revise estimate each reporting period

policy_flags.impact: Minimal difference in most cases. 'As occur' is simpler to model.

us_gaap.lower_of_cost_or_market: LCM test (cost, market = replacement cost bounded by NRV and NRV-normal profit)

ifrs.lower_of_cost_or_nrv: Cost vs NRV (estimated selling price - estimated costs to complete and sell)

policy_flags.impact: LIFO typically results in higher COGS and lower inventory in inflationary environments

us_gaap.rule: Eliminated by ASU 2015-01. No extraordinary items classification.

ifrs.rule: Prohibited. No separate classification for extraordinary items.

policy_flags.modeling_switch: Not needed — neither standard allows extraordinary items

templates.description: Five core JSON templates operationalize the specification

validation_summary.step_1: Populate RULEs for every line item calculation across IS, CFS, BS, and supporting schedules
validation_summary.step_2: Wire LINKs for every cross-statement data flow (minimum 9 high-reliability links)
validation_summary.step_3: Enforce CONSTRAINTs for all foundational accounting identities
validation_summary.step_4: Execute the SEQ manifest in the canonical build order (IS → Schedules → CFS → BS)
validation_summary.step_5: Assert DIAGs after every period to confirm structural integrity
validation_summary.step_6: Iterate SCC phases to convergence for circular references

action_plan.details: Implement the naming convention and JSON schemas for RULE, LINK, CONSTRAINT, SEQ, and DIAG artifacts
Enforce the canonical positive/negative convention at data ingestion. All IS expenses negative, all CFS outflows negative, contra accounts negative.
Build the SEQ engine following IS → Schedules → CFS → BS order. Identify SCCs (Interest-Tax-Revolver) and isolate for fixed-point iterative solving.
Centralize D&A, SBC, and tranche-level debt/PP&E waterfalls as single sources of truth feeding both IS and CFS.
Wire GAAP/IFRS toggles for leases, R&D capitalization, cash flow classifications, impairment reversal, and SBC forfeiture method.
Implement the core DIAG tests (BS balance, cash tie-out, RE continuity, WC matching, D&A consistency, debt roll-forward). Configure engine to fail-fast on any CRITICAL constraint breach.
